audit_items.proof_trace: SafeCast.toUint256() at line 392 is guarded by `if (updatedAt < dataFeedUpdatedAt)`, so it is only called when the data feed answer is actually used. When a negative answer exists but the data feed timestamp is not more recent than Data Streams, the negative answer is discarded and the uint256 Data Streams price is returned unchanged. The underlying invariant — no negative price is ever returned — holds on all code paths; the strict assertion 'always revert on negative answer' is an overstatement of the actual requirement.
